title: taraxacum dens-leonis subsp. officinale
intro: Taraxacum dens-leonis subsp. officinale, also known as the Large-leaved Dandelion, is a subspecies of the common dandelion plant, characterized by its larger leaves and more bitter flavor. This plant has been used for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, diuretic, and digestive benefits, which can aid in reducing blood pressure, improving liver function, and alleviating digestive issues. The Large-leaved Dandelion offers various health benefits, such as promoting detoxification, supporting immune function, and acting as a natural antioxidant. Herbal preparations of Taraxacum dens-leonis subsp. officinale can be consumed as tea, infusions, or tinctures, and are often used in traditional medicine to support overall well-being and health.
